meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate case study.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. Solve the 'no project ideas' dilemma by building standardized, high-value user onboarding flows for existing SaaS clients. By mastering the architecture of first-run experiences, you provide immediate revenue impact, turning a technical challenge into a high-ticket, repeatable service that clients desperately need to reduce churn.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You stare at the screen, waiting for a 'unique' project idea, while your billable hours evaporate. Meanwhile, hundreds of SaaS founders are bleeding revenue because their users drop off during the first five minutes of sign-up. The problem isn't a lack of ideas; it's a lack of focus on high-leverage problems.

By pivoting your focus to user onboarding, you stop building 'apps' and start building 'engines' that prove ROI instantly. Using modern tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect sophisticated, data-driven onboarding sequences in a fraction of the time, positioning yourself as a growth engineer rather than just another coder.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Map out the user journey. Before writing code, define the states and triggers for your onboarding flow to ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a modular user onboarding flow. Include triggers for email verification, feature tours, and profile completion states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a JSON schema for a multi-step user onboarding state machine.'

steps.title: Architect the UI Components
steps.description: Build reusable onboarding components that can be dropped into any client project. Focus on speed and accessibility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate React components for a progress-tracked onboarding modal with animation support using Framer Motion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --files ./components/onboarding --prompt 'Create a responsive, accessible onboarding modal component'.

steps.title: Implement Event Tracking
steps.description: Onboarding is useless without data. Integrate tracking to identify where users drop off in the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup event tracking hooks for the onboarding steps to log progress to a generic analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add analytics hooks to the onboarding component to track step completion rates.'

steps.title: Automate User Data Persistence
steps.description: Ensure onboarding progress is saved to the backend so users can resume if they refresh or exit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer to persist onboarding progress to a PostgreSQL database using Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a Prisma mutation for updating user_onboarding_status field.'

steps.title: Refine with A/B Testing Logic
steps.description: Wrap the onboarding flow in an A/B test flag to allow clients to optimize their conversion rates over time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add feature flag logic to the onboarding flow to toggle between two different messaging styles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement a feature flag wrapper for the onboarding component.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a standalone NPM package to reuse across client projects.
Prioritize 'Time-to-Value' by forcing the user to complete one core task before asking for profile details.
Use micro-animations to guide the eye; developers often neglect the psychological impact of UI flow.
Always include a 'Skip' button—forcing users through a flow they don't want is the fastest way to churn.
Leverage Claude Code's memory by creating a 'project context' file that defines your preferred styling libraries for every new build.
content.next_steps: Package your onboarding flow into a private repo, create a demo video, and pitch it to your existing clients as a 'Conversion Optimization' add-on.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to solve the most painful problem for SaaS founders. Stop hunting for ideas and start shipping solutions that pay. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
